task_pressure: Before broad manual mining, the system needed criteria and signs grounded in current relevant sources.
actual_trajectory: The agent had not yet built enough shared criteria to mine safely and risked producing noisy or brittle candidates.
first_failure: Candidate discovery was about to proceed without a reviewed rubric, reject taxonomy, and source-grounded evaluator fit.
consequence: Manual mining would create garbage candidates or overly narrow examples instead of a durable eval-control system.
privacy_boundary: The packet stores criteria and bounded refs only; raw session text stays local to .aoa.
existing_surface_check: Existing guide and packet schema now hold criteria, but no accepted central eval bundle is claimed for this design pressure.
candidate_state_reason: Criteria design pressure is concrete and high-value, but the rubric needs owner review before broader automation.
promotion_forbidden_until: candidate converted into trigger corpus or eval design by owner
next_step: Use this packet to seed a criteria/rubric review before any larger session-mining wave.
